steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The critical signal is in the previous sentence: “Roman numerals typically indicate the order in which the moons were named rather than the order of their proximity to Jupiter.” This clarifies what the Roman numerals actually represent—naming sequence, not physical position.

steps.title: Apply the signal to find the transition
steps.content: “Then” marks the logical consequence: given that Roman numerals indicate naming order (not proximity), it then follows that it is incorrect to assume Eirene is the fifty-seventh moon from Jupiter in terms of proximity. Moreover suggests addition rather than inference, finally suggests an endpoint in a sequence, and however signals contrast rather than consequence—none fit the logical flow. That matches choice D — D is correct.
